topics.name: Bridging Gap Activity Picture - 1
topics.summary: Students observe a village scene and answer questions to describe the setting, objects, people, and their actions, fostering observation and descriptive language skills.

topics.name: Bridging Gap Activity Picture - 2
topics.summary: Students observe a village scene depicting various occupations and interactions, answering questions for description and conversation to develop observational and communicative skills.

topics.name: Bridging Gap Activity Picture - 3
topics.summary: Students observe a picture of two boys holding a bird and answer questions to construct a story, developing narrative and imaginative skills.

topics.name: Bridging Gap Activity Picture - 4
topics.summary: Students observe a classroom scene with a teacher and students, identifying objects, activities, and imagining conversations to enhance descriptive and communicative abilities.

topics.name: Ekalavya
topics.summary: The story of Ekalavya, his dedication to learning archery, and his sacrifice for his guru, Dronacharya, followed by vocabulary, comprehension, and grammar exercises related to the story.

topics.name: Ekalavya Story: Reading and Writing
topics.summary: Reading an excerpt from the Ekalavya story and engaging in writing activities to express thoughts and create dialogue.

topics.name: Oral Skills and Vocabulary
topics.summary: Activities designed to enhance oral communication, reading comprehension, and vocabulary through discussions and word completion.

topics.name: Poem: School is Fun
topics.summary: A poem describing various enjoyable activities and aspects of school life, accompanied by illustrations.

topics.name: Comprehension, Writing Conventions, and Project Work
topics.summary: Activities including comprehension questions, correcting writing conventions, a multi-page project to make and describe a paper rabbit, and a self-assessment.

topics.name: Introduction to "The Pancake" Story
topics.summary: This topic introduces the story "The Pancake" with pre-reading activities including picture observation and listening comprehension questions.

topics.name: Reading "The Pancake" Story
topics.summary: This topic covers the full text of the story "The Pancake", detailing the adventures of a runaway pancake.

topics.name: Glossary and Comprehension
topics.summary: This topic provides a glossary of new words from "The Pancake" story and comprehension questions to assess understanding, along with discussion prompts.

topics.name: Vocabulary and Grammar: Adjectives Introduction
topics.summary: This topic focuses on expanding vocabulary related to feelings and introducing the concept of adjectives in grammar.

topics.name: Grammar: Adjectives Practice and Writing
topics.summary: This topic provides practice exercises for identifying and using adjectives, along with creative writing activities.

topics.name: Making Tea and 'oo' Words
topics.summary: This topic covers practical English skills related to making tea and a phonics exercise focusing on the 'oo' sound.

topics.name: The Monkeys Go Fasting (Story Part 1)
topics.summary: Introduction to the story "The Monkeys Go Fasting", where a group of monkeys decides to fast and then contemplates eating bananas.

topics.name: The Monkeys Go Fasting (Story Part 2)
topics.summary: Continuation of the story "The Monkeys Go Fasting", detailing the monkeys' struggle with hunger during their fast and their eventual decision to eat the bananas.

topics.name: Comprehension and Project Work
topics.summary: This section includes comprehension questions based on "The Monkeys Go Fasting" story and a project activity involving food wrappers and product information.

topics.name: Presentation, Writing Conventions, and Self-Assessment
topics.summary: This topic focuses on presentation skills, correcting sentences using proper writing conventions, and a self-assessment of the unit's learning objectives.

topics.name: What is a Tree? - Introduction
topics.summary: Introduction to the concept of trees, their importance, and initial questions about their role in the environment, accompanied by an illustration of children planting trees.

topics.name: Reading: What is a Tree? (Part 1)
topics.summary: A dialogue-based reading passage featuring Ammu, Bittu, a Farmer, and a Fisherman discussing their understanding and uses of a tree, accompanied by illustrations.

topics.name: Reading: What is a Tree? (Part 2)
topics.summary: Continuation of the dialogue from the previous page, involving a Homemaker, a Traveller, Children, a Parrot, and a Monkey, each sharing their perspective on what a tree is, with accompanying illustrations.

topics.name: Glossary and Reading Comprehension
topics.summary: Key vocabulary from the reading passage and questions to assess understanding of the story 'What is a Tree?'.

topics.name: Vocabulary: Uses and Parts of a Tree
topics.summary: Activities to explore the various uses of trees and identify the different parts of a tree and their specific applications.

topics.name: Vocabulary and Grammar: Picture-based Questions
topics.summary: An exercise to list plants/trees based on their products and a grammar activity focusing on answering questions by observing pictures from previous pages.

topics.name: Grammar: Prepositions
topics.summary: Introduction to prepositions, their function in sentences, and an activity to fill in blanks using appropriate prepositions based on a picture.

topics.name: Grammar: Writing with Prepositions
topics.summary: An activity to practice writing original sentences using prepositions based on a given picture.

topics.name: The Lion King (Part 1)
topics.summary: This topic introduces the story of The Lion King, describing his beautiful kingdom, the jungle, and its diverse inhabitants.

topics.name: The Lion King (Part 2)
topics.summary: This topic continues the story of The Lion King, describing the past happiness of the forest people and the subsequent destruction and exploitation of the forest by civilized humans.

topics.name: The Lion King: Reading Comprehension and Glossary
topics.summary: This topic concludes the story of The Lion King, followed by a glossary of new words encountered in the narrative and comprehension questions to assess understanding.

topics.name: Project Work and Self-Assessment
topics.summary: This topic provides project work related to plants and trees, encouraging students to engage in practical activities and creative writing. It also includes a self-assessment section for students to evaluate their understanding of the unit's content.

topics.name: Writing Conversations and Descriptions
topics.summary: This topic focuses on developing writing skills through creating dialogues and describing processes.

topics.name: Oral Skills, Writing Conventions, and Vocabulary
topics.summary: This topic covers developing oral communication skills, understanding writing conventions like capitalization and punctuation, and expanding vocabulary through word choice exercises.

topics.name: The Miller, His Son and His Donkey
topics.summary: This unit introduces the story "The Miller, His Son and His Donkey" through reading passages, vocabulary exercises, comprehension questions, and grammar activities focusing on collective nouns and verbs.

topics.name: Action Words and Story Writing
topics.summary: This topic focuses on identifying and using action words through matching and underlining exercises, followed by a creative writing activity to compose a paragraph about a holiday. It also includes an activity to observe pictures and write a story based on them.

topics.name: Oral Skills and Writing Conventions
topics.summary: This topic focuses on developing oral communication skills by converting a story into a play and enacting it, and improving writing skills by correcting sentences for proper grammar and punctuation.

topics.name: Be Happy (Poem and Comprehension)
topics.summary: This topic introduces the poem "Be Happy" which encourages positive emotions and actions. Students read the poem and answer comprehension questions related to its themes of happiness, bravery, and social interaction.

topics.name: Group Project on Advice
topics.summary: This topic involves a group project where students discuss and reflect on the advice they follow, both at home and at school, based on the story "The Miller, His Son and His Donkey". They prepare a group profile and present their findings.

topics.name: Fun with Words and Self-Assessment
topics.summary: This topic includes an activity to associate body parts with various actions, enhancing vocabulary and recognition skills. It concludes with a self-assessment checklist for students to evaluate their understanding and learning from the unit.

topics.name: Fudi's Desert Journey Introduction
topics.summary: This topic introduces the story "Fudi's Desert Journey" with a picture analysis and discussion questions, followed by a listening comprehension activity.

topics.name: Fudi's Desert Journey (The Story)
topics.summary: This topic presents the story of Fudi's desert journey in a comic strip format, detailing his adventures and challenges.

topics.name: Glossary
topics.summary: This topic introduces key vocabulary words from the story "Fudi's Desert Journey" with their definitions.

topics.name: Comprehension
topics.summary: This topic includes exercises to assess students' understanding of the story "Fudi's Desert Journey".

topics.name: Vocabulary
topics.summary: This topic focuses on vocabulary related to the desert and reinforces understanding of key terms from the story.

topics.name: Grammar
topics.summary: Students learn about comparative adjectives, how they are formed, and practice using them in sentences and conversations.

topics.name: Writing
topics.summary: Students practice writing a conversation based on a story scenario and describing a place they have visited using given clues.

topics.name: Oral Skills
topics.summary: Students engage in an oral activity to discuss and enact a conversation about preparing for a trip to a zoo.

topics.name: Fun with Words
topics.summary: Students read and observe the pronunciation of words, focusing on letter-sound correspondence.

topics.name: Travelling, Travelling
topics.summary: Students read a poem or song about different modes of travel, including boats, cars, planes, trains, and walking, accompanied by illustrations.

topics.name: Project Work
topics.summary: Students engage in a project to plan and discuss desired travel destinations, modes of transport, companions, and the importance of places.

topics.name: How well did I understand this unit?
topics.summary: A self-assessment activity where students evaluate their understanding of the unit's content, including listening, reading, grammar, and writing skills.

topics.name: Introduction to Honesty and Listening Activity
topics.summary: Students observe a picture related to honesty and law enforcement, answer questions, and then listen to a story about honesty to answer further questions.

topics.name: A. Reading: Honesty Story
topics.summary: Students read a story about a boy named Chinna who loses money and learns about honesty, then answer comprehension questions based on the story.

topics.name: Vocabulary: Expressions of Honesty
topics.summary: Students reflect on actions that are considered incorrect, similar to taking extra money, and add more expressions related to honesty.

topics.name: Grammar: Plural Forms and Conjunctions
topics.summary: Students learn about forming plural nouns by adding '-s' or '-es' and practice combining sentences using conjunctions like 'and' and 'but'.

topics.name: Writing: Dialogue and Recipe
topics.summary: Students practice writing by creating a dialogue based on a given situation and writing a simple recipe using provided clues.

topics.name: The Boy and the Catapult - Introduction
topics.summary: This topic introduces the story "The Boy and the Catapult" through a picture observation activity and a listening comprehension exercise.

topics.name: A. Reading - The Boy and the Catapult
topics.summary: This topic presents the full story of "The Boy and the Catapult" for students to read and comprehend.

topics.name: Glossary
topics.summary: This topic provides definitions for key vocabulary words encountered in "The Boy and the Catapult" story.

topics.name: Comprehension
topics.summary: This topic assesses students' understanding of "The Boy and the Catapult" story through a series of questions.

topics.name: Vocabulary - Young Ones of Animals/Birds
topics.summary: This topic introduces the names of young ones for various animals and birds and includes exercises to practice this vocabulary.

topics.name: Grammar - Adverbs
topics.summary: This topic introduces the concept of adverbs by identifying them in sentences from the story and providing additional examples.

topics.name: Adverb Practice - Song
topics.summary: This topic provides a song for students to read and sing, followed by an exercise to identify and write down adverbs from the song.

topics.name: Oral Skills - Picture Discussion
topics.summary: This topic encourages students to develop oral communication skills by observing a picture and discussing what is happening in it.

topics.name: Storytelling and Signboard Analysis
topics.summary: Activities involving group storytelling based on a picture, analyzing information from signboards, and reading a short story about preparing a signboard to prevent bird hunting.

topics.name: Signboard Design and Conversation Writing
topics.summary: Activities including designing a signboard to prevent bird hunting and writing a conversation between two characters, Bodh Raj and Bhisham.

topics.name: Fun with Words: Vowel Sounds
topics.summary: An activity to practice words containing the vowel combinations '-ee' and '-ea' by filling in missing letters and reading the words aloud.

topics.name: Bird Talk Poem
topics.summary: The poem "Bird Talk" which features two birds, Robin and Jay, discussing humans and their differences from birds.

topics.name: Comprehension and Self-Assessment
topics.summary: Questions to assess understanding of the "Bird Talk" poem and a self-evaluation checklist for the unit's learning objectives, including preparing a signboard and writing a conversation.

topics.name: Fun With Words
topics.summary: Students learn about compound words by reading sentences and matching word parts, and practice adding suffixes '-sh' or '-ch' to complete words.

topics.name: Superstitions Around the World
topics.summary: Students read a passage about various superstitions from different cultures, including beliefs about luck, specific actions, and objects.

topics.name: Comprehension
topics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of the "Superstitions Around the World" reading passage.

topics.name: Assertive and Interrogative Sentences
topics.summary: Students learn to differentiate between assertive (statements) and interrogative (questions) sentences and practice writing them.

topics.name: How well did I understand this unit?
topics.summary: Students self-assess their learning and understanding of the unit's content, including listening, reading, grammar, and writing skills.

topics.name: Narrative Comprehension and Creative Writing
topics.summary: This section develops narrative comprehension, dialogue writing, and creative story generation skills. It includes activities based on a story, potentially related to the theme of luck or superstition, as suggested by the footer "The Unlucky Face".

topics.name: Unit 1 Ekalavya
topics.summary: This topic presents a story about Ekalavya, Dronacharya, the Pandavas, and Kauravas, describing the ancient schooling system and the roles of gurus and shishyas.

topics.name: Unit 2 The Pancake
topics.summary: This topic presents a short story about children playing, getting hungry, and a mother making dosas (pancakes) for them.

topics.name: Unit 3 What is a Tree?
topics.summary: This topic presents a dialogue between a teacher and children about the characteristics and parts of a tree.

topics.name: Unit 4 The Miller, His Son and His Donkey
topics.summary: This topic presents a text describing a Saturday market day, announcements, and special offers for shoppers.

topics.name: Unit 5 Fudi's Desert Journey
topics.summary: This topic presents a story about a child named Fudi from a nomadic tribe and a conversation among family members about the child's beauty and name.

topics.name: Unit 6 Honesty
topics.summary: This topic presents a story about two friends, Chinna and Bablu, and Chinna's dog, focusing on a conversation that touches upon the value of honesty.

topics.name: Unit 7 The Boy and The Catapult
topics.summary: This topic presents a story about a farmer selling puppies and a boy who wants to buy one, specifically the weakest one, leading to a discussion about compassion.

topics.name: Unit 8 The Unlucky Face
topics.summary: This topic presents a story about Hirachand being scolded for coming out early, his brother's ill face, and a discussion about responsibility and a king's order.

topics.name: Appendix II About the Textbook
topics.summary: This section introduces the purpose of the textbook, outlines the academic standards it aims to achieve, and lists the types of discourses students will learn to construct, along with a description of the textbook's structure.

topics.name: Assessment
topics.summary: This section describes the two types of assessments used in the academic year: Formative and Summative assessment, detailing their implementation and marking criteria.

topics.name: Learning Outcomes
topics.summary: This section lists specific skills and abilities that learners are expected to achieve by the end of the course, covering various aspects of English language proficiency.

topics.name: General Service List - Common Words of English
topics.summary: This section provides a list of common English words, numbered from 201 to 400, intended for vocabulary building.

topics.name: NOTES
topics.summary: These pages are provided for students or teachers to write notes.

topics.name: Textbook Development Team - 2013
topics.summary: This section lists the individuals involved in the development of the textbook, including advisors, editors, contributors, illustrators, and layout designers.

topics.name: Textbook Publication Team
topics.summary: This topic lists the key personnel and their roles within the Textbook Publication Team, including directors from SCERT and Govt. Textbook Press, and a Professor & Head of Department from the Curriculum and Textbooks Department.

topics.name: QRCODETEAM
topics.summary: This topic introduces the QR Code Team and provides a QR code for accessing related digital content or information.

subtopics.name: Picture Description
subtopics.summary: Students describe the elements within a given picture, including the place, things, people, and their actions.

subtopics.name: Picture Description
subtopics.summary: Students describe the elements within a given picture, including the place, things, people, and their actions.

subtopics.name: Interactive Conversation
subtopics.summary: Students imagine and practice conversations based on the interactions depicted in the picture.

subtopics.name: Story Building from Picture
subtopics.summary: Students create a narrative based on visual cues, including past, present, and future events, and imagine dialogue and a title for the story.

subtopics.name: Picture Description
subtopics.summary: Students describe the elements within a given picture, including the place, things, people, and their actions.

subtopics.name: Interactive Conversation
subtopics.summary: Students identify characters and imagine a conversation between them, including who speaks first and their responses.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Ekalavya
subtopics.summary: Introduction to the story of Ekalavya through a picture and initial questions for discussion and listening comprehension.

subtopics.name: Ekalavya Story - Part 1
subtopics.summary: Reading the first part of the story of Ekalavya, introducing Dronacharya, the Pandavas, the Kauravas, Arjuna, and Ekalavya's desire to learn archery.

subtopics.name: Ekalavya Story - Part 2
subtopics.summary: Continuation of the Ekalavya story, detailing his practice of archery and the incident with the dog.

subtopics.name: Ekalavya Story - Part 3
subtopics.summary: Conclusion of the Ekalavya story, focusing on Dronacharya's promise to Arjuna, the concept of Guru Dakshina, and Ekalavya's sacrifice.

subtopics.name: Glossary and Comprehension
subtopics.summary: A glossary of new words from the Ekalavya story, followed by comprehension questions and an activity to identify Ekalavya's qualities.

subtopics.name: Qualities of a Good Student and School Activities
subtopics.summary: Activities to discuss and write about the qualities of a good student and to identify and list subjects and activities learned at school.

subtopics.name: Naming Words (Nouns)
subtopics.summary: Introduction to naming words (nouns) with examples from the Ekalavya story and an exercise to identify nouns.

subtopics.name: Pronouns
subtopics.summary: Introduction to pronouns as words used instead of naming words, with examples and an exercise to replace nouns with suitable pronouns, including a song activity.

subtopics.name: Oral Skills
subtopics.summary: Reading a passage, discussing family members' traits, and role-playing introductions based on a given profile.

subtopics.name: Fun with Words
subtopics.summary: A vocabulary exercise to complete words by adding the suffixes '-or' or '-er'.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Adjectives
subtopics.summary: Explains what adjectives are and how they describe persons, places, things, and their qualities.

subtopics.name: Glossary
subtopics.summary: Definitions of key terms 'homemaker' and 'fire wood' from the reading passage.

subtopics.name: Reading: Introduction to the Lion King's Kingdom
subtopics.summary: Students read the first part of the story, learning about the Lion King and the natural beauty and creatures of his forest kingdom.

subtopics.name: Reading: The Forest's Past and Present
subtopics.summary: Students read about the contrast between the forest's thriving past and its current state of decline due to human actions, as narrated by the Lion King.

subtopics.name: Reading: The Lion King's Lament
subtopics.summary: Students read the final part of the story where the Lion King expresses his sorrow and anger over the destruction of his kingdom by hunters and smugglers.

subtopics.name: Glossary for The Lion King
subtopics.summary: Students learn the meanings of new vocabulary words from the story, including 'captured', 'hill tribes', 'civilized', 'resources', 'benefits', and 'subjects'.

subtopics.name: Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of the story "The Lion King".

subtopics.name: Project Work: Plant/Tree Activities
subtopics.summary: Students are given project tasks such as choosing a plant/tree, collecting leaves/flowers, and writing about a tree, fostering observation and writing skills.

subtopics.name: Self-Assessment of Unit Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students use a tick-box format to assess their comprehension of the unit's key topics, including listening, reading, understanding prepositions, and writing conversations and descriptions.

subtopics.name: Reading the story: The Miller, His Son and His Donkey (Part 1)
subtopics.summary: The first part of the story "The Miller, His Son and His Donkey" is presented for reading comprehension.

subtopics.name: Reading the story: The Miller, His Son and His Donkey (Part 2)
subtopics.summary: The second part of the story "The Miller, His Son and His Donkey" is presented for reading comprehension.

subtopics.name: Glossary for "The Miller, His Son and His Donkey"
subtopics.summary: A list of new vocabulary words from the story with their meanings.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Collective Nouns
subtopics.summary: This section introduces the concept of collective nouns with examples and illustrations.

subtopics.name: Examples of Collective Nouns
subtopics.summary: Further examples of collective nouns are provided with corresponding images.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Verbs / Action Words
subtopics.summary: This section defines verbs as action words and provides examples from the story.

subtopics.name: Matching Action Words
subtopics.summary: Students match nouns with appropriate action words.

subtopics.name: Identifying Regular Actions
subtopics.summary: Students read a list of actions and underline those they perform regularly.

subtopics.name: Writing about a Holiday
subtopics.summary: Students write a short paragraph describing their activities on a holiday, using action words.

subtopics.name: Picture-Based Story Writing
subtopics.summary: Students observe a sequence of pictures featuring a mouse, answer comprehension questions about them, and then write a complete story based on the visuals.

subtopics.name: Story to Play Enactment
subtopics.summary: Students read "The Miller, His Son and His Donkey" story again, convert it into a play, assign characters, practice dialogues, and enact it in the classroom, guided by questions.

subtopics.name: Sentence Correction
subtopics.summary: Students rewrite given sentences, applying correct usage of capital letters, commas, full stops, and question marks, and correcting spelling errors.

subtopics.name: Reading the Poem "Be Happy"
subtopics.summary: Students read the poem "Be Happy" which describes various emotions and social interactions, promoting a positive outlook.

subtopics.name: Poem Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions based on the poem "Be Happy" to demonstrate their understanding of its message about desirable qualities and personal aspirations.

subtopics.name: Group Discussion on Advice
subtopics.summary: Students discuss whose advice they follow at home and at school, and why, relating it to the story of the miller and his son.

subtopics.name: Group Profile Creation
subtopics.summary: Students fill in a table to create a profile of their group, detailing whose advice each classmate follows at home and at school.

subtopics.name: Group Profile Presentation
subtopics.summary: Students present their group's profile to the class, explaining whose advice most members follow and providing reasons.

subtopics.name: Actions with Body Parts
subtopics.summary: Students identify and associate various actions with specific body parts (hands, legs, eyes, mouth, nose).

subtopics.name: Unit Self-Assessment
subtopics.summary: Students evaluate their understanding of the unit's content, including listening, reading comprehension of the story and poem, verb usage, and writing skills, by ticking appropriate boxes.

subtopics.name: Picture Analysis and Discussion
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a picture related to a desert journey and answer questions based on their observations and general knowledge.

subtopics.name: Listening Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a conversation read by the teacher and answer questions to test their comprehension.

subtopics.name: Reading the Story
subtopics.summary: Students read the comic strip story about Fudi's journey through the desert, encountering various situations and overcoming difficulties.

subtopics.name: Learning New Words
subtopics.summary: Students learn the meanings of words such as tribe, caravan, pouch, rescue, pirates, gallop, mirage, quick sand, oasis, and compass.

subtopics.name: Answering Story Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer direct questions about the plot, characters, and events in "Fudi's Desert Journey".

subtopics.name: Identifying Speakers
subtopics.summary: Students identify who spoke specific sentences from the story and to whom they were spoken.

subtopics.name: Sequencing Events
subtopics.summary: Students arrange a list of difficulties faced by Fudi in the correct chronological order as they occurred in the story.

subtopics.name: Desert-Related Words
subtopics.summary: Students identify and list words and phrases from the story that are related to the desert environment.

subtopics.name: Matching Descriptions to Words
subtopics.summary: Students match given descriptions to appropriate vocabulary words or phrases from a provided list.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Comparatives
subtopics.summary: Students read a sentence from the story and learn about the comparative forms of adjectives like 'bigger', 'better', and 'older', understanding their meaning and formation rules (adding -er, doubling letters).

subtopics.name: Writing a Conversation
subtopics.summary: Students are given a scenario from the story (Fudi's father asking questions after his journey) and are asked to write a possible conversation between Fudi and his father.

subtopics.name: Describing a Visited Place
subtopics.summary: Students are guided to write a description of a place or village they have visited, using specific questions as clues (e.g., where, when, what seen, what liked, special things).

subtopics.name: Zoo Trip Discussion and Enactment
subtopics.summary: Students are asked to sit in pairs and enact a conversation about how they would prepare for a trip to a zoo, considering what to take, care needed, and companions.

subtopics.name: Pronunciation Observation
subtopics.summary: Students are presented with a table of words and individual letters, instructed to read them and observe their pronunciation, likely focusing on initial sounds or specific letter sounds.

subtopics.name: Poem/Song: Travelling, Travelling
subtopics.summary: A poem or song describing various ways of travelling (by boat, car, plane, train, and foot) with simple rhyming verses and corresponding illustrations for each mode of transport.

subtopics.name: Answering Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions related to the "Travelling, Travelling" poem, such as identifying the poet, describing why travelling is fun, and expressing personal travel preferences.

subtopics.name: Matching Travel Phrases
subtopics.summary: Students match phrases describing travel actions (e.g., "Row your boat") with appropriate descriptive phrases (e.g., "round the lake", "for a merry cruise").

subtopics.name: Travel Planning and Discussion
subtopics.summary: Students list places they would like to visit, complete a table detailing how they would go there, with whom, and the importance of the place, followed by group discussion and chart preparation.

subtopics.name: Unit Self-Assessment
subtopics.summary: Students use a checklist to self-evaluate their comprehension of conversations, the 'Fudi’s Desert Journey' text, usage of comparatives, enjoyment of the 'Travelling, Travelling' song, and ability to write a conversation and a place description.

subtopics.name: Picture Observation and Discussion
subtopics.summary: Students analyze a picture depicting a scene involving a policeman and a thief, then discuss related questions about law and order.

subtopics.name: Listening Comprehension: The Story of Chinna
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story read by the teacher (text in Appendix-I) and answer specific questions about the narrative.

subtopics.name: Reading the Story "Honesty"
subtopics.summary: Students read the story about Chinna, Kamala, and the missing money, and their visit to the market.

subtopics.name: Glossary
subtopics.summary: Students learn the meanings of new words encountered in the story: gently, wipe, hereafter.

subtopics.name: Story Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of the story's plot, characters' feelings, and moral lessons.

subtopics.name: Identifying Incorrect Actions
subtopics.summary: Students identify and list other actions that are considered incorrect, building on the concept of honesty from the story.

subtopics.name: Forming Plural Nouns
subtopics.summary: Students observe examples of singular and plural nouns and practice writing the plural forms by adding '-s' or '-es'.

subtopics.name: Using Conjunctions ('and', 'but')
subtopics.summary: Students learn how to combine two sentences into one using conjunctions like 'and' and 'but', with examples and practice exercises.

subtopics.name: Writing a Conversation
subtopics.summary: Students write a possible conversation between Chinna and his mother based on the story's context of Chinna losing his money.

subtopics.name: Writing a Recipe
subtopics.summary: Students write a simple recipe for lemon juice, listing ingredients and steps using given clues.

subtopics.name: Picture Observation and Discussion
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture depicting children interacting with birds and answer questions based on their observations.

subtopics.name: Listening Comprehension
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a text (from Appendix-1) and answer questions to demonstrate their understanding.

subtopics.name: Story Reading
subtopics.summary: Students read the narrative about Bodh Raj and his interaction with birds, initially with a catapult and later with kindness.

subtopics.name: Vocabulary Definitions
subtopics.summary: Students learn the meanings of words like pebble, catapult, excellent, peeping, flutter, shrieking, beam, stepped down, straw, peered, perched, and garage.

subtopics.name: Story Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions about the plot, character development, and moral message of the story.

subtopics.name: Names of Young Animals and Birds
subtopics.summary: Students learn and recognize the specific terms for the young of different animals and birds, such as eaglet, gosling, calf, kid, cub, duckling, lamb, piglet, owlet, puppy, kitten, and chick.

subtopics.name: Birds I Know Concept Map
subtopics.summary: Students complete a concept map by listing different birds they know.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Adverbs
subtopics.summary: Students learn that adverbs are words that describe verbs, using examples like "rarely" and "quickly" from the story.

subtopics.name: Adverb Examples
subtopics.summary: Students are given additional examples of adverbs in sentences to reinforce their understanding.

subtopics.name: Song Recitation
subtopics.summary: Students read and sing a song that contains various adverbs.

subtopics.name: Adverb Identification and Writing
subtopics.summary: Students identify adverbs within the song and write them down.

subtopics.name: Picture Observation and Discussion
subtopics.summary: Students look at a picture and verbally describe and discuss the activities depicted.

subtopics.name: Glossary
subtopics.summary: Definitions for key terms found in the "Bird Talk" poem, specifically "Jay" and "beetles".

subtopics.name: Compound Words
subtopics.summary: Students identify and form compound words by combining two smaller words.

subtopics.name: Suffixes '-sh' and '-ch'
subtopics.summary: Students practice completing words by adding the correct '-sh' or '-ch' suffix.

subtopics.name: Reading about Global Superstitions
subtopics.summary: Students read a text describing different superstitions from countries like Brazil, Australia, Ireland, Hawaii, and general beliefs.

subtopics.name: Discussing Lucky and Unlucky Beliefs
subtopics.summary: Students are prompted to discuss what they consider lucky and unlucky based on the reading and their own knowledge.

subtopics.name: Answering Comprehension Questions
subtopics.summary: Students respond to questions about the superstitions mentioned in the passage and reflect on local superstitions.

subtopics.name: Identifying Sentence Types
subtopics.summary: Students learn to recognize assertive and interrogative sentences using examples from an essay.

subtopics.name: Constructing Sentences
subtopics.summary: Students practice writing assertive and interrogative sentences based on an essay and a given picture.

subtopics.name: Self-Reflection on Learning
subtopics.summary: Students evaluate their comprehension of stories and texts, understanding of past tense, and ability to write conversations and thoughts.

subtopics.name: Ekalavya's Story
subtopics.summary: Students listen to the story of Ekalavya, a student who learned various subjects and skills, including fighting and shooting, and his relationship with Dronacharya, the Pandavas, and Kauravas.

subtopics.name: The Story of The Pancake
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story about children enjoying a holiday, playing games like hide and seek, skipping, and hopscotch, and then returning home hungry for dosas.

subtopics.name: Dialogue about a Tree
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a conversation where Teacher Sujatha asks children what a tree is, and they identify its parts like the stem, leaves, and branches.

subtopics.name: Market Day Story
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a text about a busy Saturday market day, with bells ringing, announcements being made, and various goods and special offers available for purchase.

subtopics.name: Fudi's Story
subtopics.summary: Students listen to the story of Fudi, a child born into a nomadic tribe, and a dialogue where family members express happiness and admiration for the child, discussing their appearance and name.

subtopics.name: The Story of Honesty
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story about Chinna and Bablu playing, where Bablu asks to play with Chinna's dog, Tommy, and Chinna's thoughts about the dog's bells being nice.

subtopics.name: The Story of The Boy and The Catapult
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story about a farmer selling puppies and a boy named Vamsi who wants to buy a puppy. The boy shows interest in the weakest puppy, leading to a conversation with the farmer.

subtopics.name: The Story of The Unlucky Face
subtopics.summary: Students listen to a story where Hirachand is questioned about coming out early, his brother's unwell appearance, and a discussion about who is responsible and a potential order from the king.

subtopics.name: The textbook for class IV has been developed keeping the following as the learning outcomes expected from the learners at the end of the academic year.
subtopics.summary: Explains that the textbook is developed to meet specific learning outcomes for students by the end of the academic year.

subtopics.name: Academic Standards
subtopics.summary: Lists six broad categories of academic standards children are expected to achieve: Listening and Responding, Reading Comprehension, Conventions of Writing, Vocabulary, Grammar, and Creative Expression (Oral and Written Discourses).

subtopics.name: The children should listen to, read and construct the following discourses both orally and in the written form.
subtopics.summary: Details ten types of discourses students should be able to construct, including descriptions, conversations, stories/narratives, rhymes/songs, recipes, slogans, posters, letters, skits/dramas, and short essays.

subtopics.name: This textbook contains eight theme based units which are within the experiential orbit of the children. Each unit has a listening passage and two reading texts (A) and (B) along with textual exercises covering vocabulary, grammar, writing, project work etc.
subtopics.summary: Describes the textbook's structure, mentioning eight theme-based units, listening passages, and reading texts (A) and (B) with textual exercises covering vocabulary, grammar, writing, and project work.

subtopics.name: There are two types of assessments.
subtopics.summary: Introduces the two main types of assessments: Formative assessment and Summative assessment.

subtopics.name: Formative assessment
subtopics.summary: Explains that formative assessment is conducted four times in an academic year, based on observation, notebooks, project work, and slip tests, with specific mark allocations and criteria for awarding marks.

subtopics.name: Summative Assessment
subtopics.summary: Describes summative assessments as two conducted in an academic year, covering all academic standards, with marks allotted for oral and written tests.

subtopics.name: The learner....
subtopics.summary: A comprehensive list of what the learner will be able to do, including responding to instructions/questions, enacting roles, speaking on familiar topics, presenting orally, reading aloud, reading subtitles/news, reading printed script, describing events/places, using punctuation, writing informal letters, using linkers, using nouns/verbs/adjectives/prepositions, and inferring meaning from context/dictionary.

subtopics.name: 201 to 400 Words (Part 1)
subtopics.summary: A list of common English words from 201 to 296, presented in a tabular format.

subtopics.name: 201 to 400 Words (Part 2)
subtopics.summary: A continuation of the list of common English words from 297 to 400, presented in a tabular format.

subtopics.summary: A blank page with lines for writing notes.

subtopics.summary: A blank page with lines for writing notes.

subtopics.summary: Lists the names and roles of the advisors, editors, contributors, illustrators, and layout designer for the Class IV textbook developed in 2013.

The words "bigger, better and older" are used to describe the two things that are compared in the sentence. The word "bigger" is used to describe the size of the carrots, and the word "older" is used to describe the age of the carrots.

A group of monkeys lived in a forest. One day, they went out to buy food.
"Before we begin, I think we should keep the food ready with which we'll eat," said the monkey who was in charge of the group. The young monkeys were sent in search of food. [IMAGE] Grammar
1. Read the following sentence from the story.
"In the town very far, the carrots became bigger, the good better and Fufu grew older."
The words "bigger, better and older" are used to describe two things. We use the word "bigger" to describe the big things. We use the word "older" to describe the old things. We also use the quality and value words "better" and "good".
These words are formed by adding "ed" or "edible" to the last word. We also use the word "more" to describe the more things. We also use the word "less" to describe the less things. Some times a new word may also be used as a comparative.
Example:
big
bigger
good
better
Find some more comparative from the story and write them in your note book.

[IMAGE] A. Reading
The Boy and the Catapult
The oddest fellow in my class was Bodd Raj. He always had a catapult in his hand. He was an excellent shooter. He could round under 1000, take aim and fire with precision. He was a very clever boy. The other children in the class were from his class. He was a tall boy with a full head of hair.
I lived with my parents in an old bungalow by the outskirts of the city. I used to play with my friends nearby every day. First me, then Bodd Raj found it a good place to play.
Behind the bungalow there was a godswill. This noon had become a regular sight for me. We used to go there every day.
The next time Bodd Raj called, he went straight to the godswill. As usual, I had gone to the same place. He was waiting for me next near the godswill.
"The myna's little one is playing there," said Bodd Raj to me. I noticed two tiny birds perched out of the nest.
Bodd Raj was a catapult and I found a small stick and a stone and I threw it at him. He stopped him. Suddenly, a
small bird landed on his head.

Birds I Know
Read the following sentences taken from the story.
The fish quickly came to the surface.
Bibi, Raj quickly ran and pulled up a table.
In the fish sentence, the verb quickly talks about the verb come. In the scene sentence, the verb quickly talks about the verb run. Such words are called auxiliary verbs.
Here are some more examples:
Raj ran very fast.
He spoke in low loudly.
Come soon, the bell is ringing.
